desc: Diani Beach is one of East Africa's easier coast add-ons if you want long white sand, warm water and resort comfort without giving up access to reefs, kite spots and a little wildlife interest inland. It works best as a laid-back beach base with selective outings, because tides, marine conditions and transfer times all matter more than the map initially suggests.

todo.name: Diani Beach lagoon
todo.desc: Use tide timing for swims, reef walks and long beach strolls on the white-sand strip.

todo.name: Colobus conservation stop
todo.desc: Visit or support responsible conservation work focused on the area's colobus monkeys.

todo.name: Kisite-Mpunguti marine trip
todo.desc: Book a reputable operator for reef snorkelling and dolphin-aware boat practices.

todo.name: Kongo River sunset
todo.desc: Head toward the river mouth for a quieter late-day view away from the central resort strip.

todo.name: Kaya Kinondo Sacred Forest
todo.desc: Add a guided forest visit for a break from the beach and a better sense of local Mijikenda heritage.

todo.name: Galu beach walk
todo.desc: Use the southern end of the coast for a longer, quieter sand walk when the central resort area feels too busy.

wifi.note: Usually fine in established resorts, cowork-friendly cafes and villa compounds, but beach stretches, marine trips and some smaller boutique stays are patchier than the main strip suggests.

prac.fltNote: Diani has no practical direct UK routing. Most travellers either connect onto Ukunda for the shortest final transfer or fly into Mombasa and finish by road to the south coast, which can still take longer than it looks once traffic stacks up. UK demand is strongest in winter-sun periods, Christmas and safari-plus-beach combinations rather than evenly across the year.
prac.visa: Kenya requires advance electronic travel authorisation for most British visitors, and GOV.UK says the local authorities recommend applying at least 2 weeks before departure.
alerts: Reef walking needs footwear and tide awareness.
Final transfers from Mombasa can be slow even when the straight-line distance looks modest.
Use reputable operators for marine wildlife trips.
Beach quality changes with tides, sea grass and wind, so the sea can look very different from brochure photos.

sweet: July to September are usually the cleanest window for beach time, marine trips and lower-humidity days, while January and February are strong if you want hotter winter-sun weather.
